text.heading: What the Spirit Said
text.p: On the seventh second of the seventh minute of the seventh hour, the seventh day of the seventh week, summing up the seventh month metaphysically proven to be the astronomical seventh year of the pregnancy of the moon. Upon the seventh toll of the bell hanging at the cathedral tower- it was said that on that day, it was the hand of the seventh spirit that tolled the bell- there, ushered in by the seventh crow of the fattest cock bought on the seventh market day- the seventh child (in chicken children) of his mother hen-. Upon the exact seventh toll of the bell, a young man of about twenty something years, say seven and twenty, was seen in the company of some fellows grieving, defying the elemental forces of JulyŌĆÖs wetty chill, nearly giving up the ghost and would have very willingly done so being beside himself with grief. The down pour was heavy. ŌĆśHis loss is colossalŌĆÖ people would say, sighing and shaking their heads. Afterwards they recommended him to the mercies of the lord- in prayer. They all matched, gloomy and wet in honour of the departed. ŌĆśWould he survive? Can he survive this ? ŌĆś They would ask themselves in silence as they moved on with their lives.

Sometime later, a period of unidentified duration after telling the story of this incident for its million record time passed, (you must pardon me for he has told this story so many times without losing its flavour that I can only say it has been told over a million times) he remained still, holding a strong fastened gaze at the image before him.
They were in church and the choir had intoned the beautiful song ŌĆśHere we areŌĆÖ
They sang it with gusto;
ŌĆśHere we are all together as we sing our song, joyfully
Here we are, joined together as we pray weŌĆÖll always beŌĆ”ŌĆÖ

As they were at ŌĆśŌĆ”brothers join me nowŌĆ”ŌĆÖ when a toddler broke loose from its motherŌĆÖs grip and ran into the church central isle laughing with the freedom of the wind; careless and very free. Everyone turned to look at him, being initially in its own world, it suddenly became aware of multiple eyes centered on him, he stopped, looked around and began to cry very hysterically, with same abandonment as his laughter. The mother, hiding her embarrassment, came forward, carried him, went out of the church to succour him, and the church continued like nothing happened. Outside, people were scattered. The baby refused every form of appeasement until its mother, with remarkable embarrassment and controlled annoyance, brought out her ripe bulging breast and placed it in the childŌĆÖs mouth. The happy one brought out both hands and held the breast, it smoothly sulked with one hand on the breast he was sulking while it administratively placed the other hand on the other breast for protective measures. He was with her.

But that church was different from this one here. He is not with her. ŌĆśdust thou art, dust thou returnestŌĆÖ the priest had said and waving his hand, he had said again ŌĆÖinomini patre, et filio, et spiritu santus AmenŌĆ”. The lord giveth, the lord taketh, blessed be the name of the lordŌĆÖ. In his brief oration the clergyman had said ŌĆśŌĆ”we must obey the dictates of the spiritŌĆ” it is what the spirit saidŌĆ” that, must come to pass, that, we must obey.ŌĆÖ
After doing his ŌĆśdust to dustŌĆÖ the one whose loss was the greatest went out of the church cemetery barely making his way to his carŌĆ”
It was not yet noon.

He struggled with his eyes to conceptualize what the exact picture of the image was. Like they would say, he was just between sleeping and waking. Somnambulism had not yet set in. It was the 5am sleep that is termed the sweetest. He must have been in a trance, some kind of hallucination or a strange transformation. He was quite aware that he was standing on an unfamiliar terrain. He struggled to posses back the grip of reality that he was losing. He felt it; he was losing touch. Tried all he could to shake it off, his delirium persisted. He must have embarked on a journey to a deadly silent graveyard. The air was chilly and monstrously quiet.
This is only one of those things that must find a way of happening. It is the stifled cry that finally escapes the throat no matter how hard you try to stop it.

He was being swallowed by a sloppy abyss of vacuum. He was sinking into an ocean of emptiness. All around him was space, craftily solemn void; nothing else but a vastness of empty whitish stretch, no, I beg your pardon, it is not exactly white but colourless vacuum and transcendent refulgence. Solid, insoluble but empty-white-charred rocks choked him. He had suddenly become claustrophobic in an empty space. He choked and coughed till he held his heart to helplessly abate the pains that promised to tear it to shreds.
He staggered and laughed tears, not really laughing at his agoraphobia but his ability in this dreadful dungeon to summon up tearful laughter till he could breathe no more.

You know, it is that kind of laughter that is better if you cried out real loud with tears running freely and all your members shaking like a grinder. If it was a part of the raging confusion it was clearly unclear. But he sat there vibrating in deep choking ŌĆśsomethingŌĆÖ.
ŌĆśSurely I must configure this image today; I am not going to give up. I must know what this image is, which has been imprinted indelibly on my mindŌĆÖ.
He managed to whisper to his almost incapacitated self.
It was all he could say.

He held on, refusing to bat an eyelid in other not to lose the grip he had managed to hold on this nerve wreaking image. What is tormenting him? I, I was not aware. Should he lose the image, he would be so sad from the look of things. I do not think he could go through the process of recapturing this woozy-blurry image again.
ŌĆśIt wonŌĆÖt serve me any good going through this process again, I like keeping the records straight. I like to pay my dues.ŌĆÖ
Ekwedike said again to himself.
He thought again to himself.

There was a kind of space that encircled him interchanging into a triangular orbit to help the confusion. He felt sure that he was alone, but suddenly and slowly, some grassroot fear took hold of him. He felt the goose pimples stand erect all over his body for an eternal split second.

The kind of fear that sends cold shivers down oneŌĆÖs spine, especially on dark village roads where there is a crossroad, a cold bath in the river on a harmattan morning or the sudden sight of a serpent. He was abandoned to uncertainty when he realized to his chagrin that indeed there were two people in the room. He was split in two. One to fear, the other to uncertainty.
Upon this shuddering revelation, showers of cold sweat escaped his pores and trickled down his bodyŌĆ”
ŌĆ”Yet he was alone! All alone to himself. Alone and lost in his misery from miserably beautiful life. He wasnŌĆÖt just confused and vexed but he was also deeply amazed.
ŌĆśThe undeniable sureness that reality could not blot away told me that I was not alone, yet it was I, only I and I alone.ŌĆÖ
He later would say.

There was an image with a heavy club heading towards him, the club was directly aimed at his head, and just as he made for the closest exit which was between him and his adversary, he changed his mind and rushed towards the image- headlong. There and then he noticed that two distinct individuals were fighting to make use of a single life. Fighting to outdo each other. Fighting to gain supreme control to the otherŌĆÖs disappointment and misfortune. There was a fierce battle going on.
The battle ground? - the mind.
The weapons? ŌĆō memory.
The warlord, victim or victor? ŌĆō ONESELF.

One conqueror-master must emerge to enslave the other and I am the sole witness to this emancipation. Does it matter anyway? Thugery, slavery, insanity and brutality are all the stories of man and his world.

The great divide between the weak and the strong, class distinction had always been by force. He saw this as the images warred. It is the dance of the warriors. An enchanting macabre that calls the Dikei to the square. Just as he was rushing headlong to the image, a few meters before he reached the image with the raised club, I heard a shattering noise and there was total silence, darkness, blackoutŌĆ”
He is EKWEDIKE1The call of a warrior and this is his story:
text.heading: Within
text.p: ŌĆśŌĆ”It was I and only I alone; I dare not bother about illusions and mirages for I know who I am. I am Ekwedike the son of a warrior from the line of great men. I have seen battles and fought many. Great men have fallen to drunken slumber of slaughter at my feet. It is I Ekwedike the son of Anunuebe, I shall not stumble and fall.2A Proverb. For each of the proverbs, their deeper meanings are in the knowledge of the Igbo language. I shall not drown in the river our fathers swam and drank.3A Proverb. I still shall not stop where they stopped: for I shall say to the sons of the rising generation that I, Ekwedike is indeed a warrior of sort. My escapades shall forever bless the rising sun and dare the moonlit stories.ŌĆÖ
Upon my miraculous resuscitation, these were my fervent lamentations and words of courage as I faced this hideous image that besieged me.

No! I was not afraid for there has not been anything that could ever terrify the lion in the jungle let alone in his den; yet I was afraid and soaked to my heart and spirit with sweat, yes, sweat of fear for the ŌĆśwould beŌĆÖ which I know not when it would be. My head was still swimming in the wrecked abyss sentenced upon it by the club of the image. The cloak of my virtuous apparel held a picture of a transcending brightness unfathomable to me. This development I could not explain. Yet I battled this new battle that has brought its frontier to my home, not just to my home but to me, to my very person, into my inner chambers; to the very essence of my being. ŌĆśTufia-kwa!iiTufia kwa- a sound of rejection made with the mouth meaning I forbid or God forbid. The son of a lion can never eat grass. No, mba nu.iiiMba nu- No of course The okro vegetable does not grow taller than he that planted it to prevent a harvest.ŌĆÖ4A proverb

I kept awakening courageous summons with familiar outbursts. I sighed and wiped away the tears quickly building up in my eyes. Is this the exit of the warrior? In whose ears shall these uproarious stories be poured? I wonderedŌĆ”

ŌĆ”In my grief, the heavens opened and I took steps back to the battles of Igwenga, that saw only I Ekwedike Ikenga coming back home safely with my Oja5Dagger. intact on my waist. What happened to others is only a thing to hold sacred. Only the mystagogue can tell them to the initiates as a secret story about the great warriors of our land. A hunter does not say all the things he sees in the forests.6A proverb The only useful saying is that it was only I Ekwedike Ikenga who came back alive and healthy.

I knew too well that the frog does not come out on a sunny day for naught.7A proverb I need not the oracle to know what chased this frog of images out upon me on a day like this. I know why they came rushing like a swam of locusts. I know what is chasing them. I also know that the lion being the king of the jungle does not make it the biggest animal in the wilds.

Upon this knowledge, I spoke to the image that had held me captive all day as though I spoke to myself, I called it by my name and spoke as though I spoke to myself; for I knew I was alone but not really alone.

ŌĆ£Listen to me Utobi Ekwedike Ikenga8Ikenga- Symbol of authority and power for the IgbosŌĆ? was all I could say before busting out in a fervent Ogu9Ogu- the true deified conscience that symbolises purity of heart.; talking to myself in an effort to talk to the image.
I went on; ŌĆ£you must forget the past and face the present keeping the future in view.ŌĆ?
